description: §8.1.1 / §44.7 — a `?{}` SQL block whose server function has NO `db=` in any ancestor `<program>` (and the file is not a §44.7.1 module-with-db-context) fires E-SQL-004. Before the fix, codegen silently emitted `new SQL(":memory:")` (an ephemeral empty store → runtime data loss); the SHALL-error was cataloged but never fired.
rationale: §44.2 driver resolution step 4: `If no db= found, emit E-SQL-004`. `getUsers` escalates to the server boundary by its `?{}` body; with no `<program db=>` / `<db src=>` scope its `_scrml_sql` handle has no connection, so E-SQL-004 fires (fail-closed) instead of the silent :memory: fallback.
